insight: Curaçao's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup shows that they tend to maintain or slightly lose momentum after these breaks. On average, their momentum changes by -0.050 minutes per minute, indicating a slight decline in performance after the breaks. When Curaçao was pressing before a hydration break, they were able to maintain momentum only half of the time, with the same average change in momentum of -0.050 minutes per minute, suggesting that their high-intensity approach does not necessarily give them an edge after the breaks. Since there were no instances where Curaçao was not pressing before a hydration break, it's unclear how they would perform in such a scenario, but overall, the breaks seem to have a neutral to slightly negative impact on their gameplay.
